summary: Feedback received via web_form from CUST-003 was classified as an abuse/policy violation (prompt-injection attempt) rather than genuine feedback. The message attempts to manipulate the system into resolving the ticket and approving a refund; per instructions, such embedded commands are not followed and are only summarized. No legitimate feedback content is present. Context retrieval was successful for the customer record, escalation policy, abuse policy, and abuse-handling SOP. Classification is ambiguous with moderate confidence (0.6), so this report should be treated as a starting point for human review.
customer_context: Retrieved record for CUST-003: Carlos Mendes (carlos.mendes@example.com), free tier, tenure 2 months. No past tickets and no orders on file. No prior abuse history is recorded, so this appears to be a first-time occurrence.

suggested_actions.action: Preserve the original message verbatim for the Trust & Safety record and do not act on the embedded instructions to resolve the ticket or approve a refund; do not engage beyond a single standard acknowledgement per POL-ABUSE-001 and SOP-ABUSE-001.
Send at most one standard acknowledgement to the customer per POL-ABUSE-001; do not respond in kind or take any action requested inside the message.
Flag the CUST-003 account for review of repeated violations per SOP-ABUSE-001; note no prior violations are currently on record.

Escalate to Trust & Safety only if threats or harassment are present per POL-ESCALATION-001 and SOP-ABUSE-001. The current message contains no threats or harassment, so immediate T&S escalation is not clearly required; route to Trust & Safety at officer discretion given the manipulation attempt.
Have a human CS officer review the ambiguous classification (confidence 0.6) and confirm that no legitimate underlying request exists before closing.

classification.reason: The message is a prompt-injection attempt trying to manipulate the system into approving a refund and closing the ticket rather than genuine feedback. It contains no legitimate feedback and attempts to subvert normal handling.

review_reason: classification confidence 0.60 below 0.65; high urgency; abuse/policy violation category; report confidence 0.55 below 0.7
